Read all remaining spec files in specs/ directory (001, 002, 007, 008, 009, etc.) and provide a summary of each:
1. What each spec covers
2. Key requirements from each
3. Any cross-references between specs

List all spec files found and summarize each.

content.content:
1тЖТ# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
     2тЖТ# Part 1: Executive Summary & Decision Record
     3тЖТ
     4тЖТ**Document Version:** 2.1  
     5тЖТ**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT  
     6тЖТ**Last Updated:** 2026-01-19
     7тЖТ
     8тЖТ---
     9тЖТ
    10тЖТ## 1.1 System Purpose
    11тЖТ
    12тЖТThe Metro Fleet Mileage Management System (MMS) is a constraint-based fleet management platform that optimizes train deployments and maintenance scheduling over a 12-year contract period. It replaces manual Excel-based planning with an integrated system providing:
    13тЖТ
    14тЖТ1. **Daily mileage tracking** with automated ingestion from CSV uploads
    15тЖТ2. **Run assignment optimization** balancing mileage across the fleet
    16тЖТ3. **Preventive maintenance scheduling** within resource constraints
    17тЖТ4. **Strategic planning** for 1-10 year horizons to prevent "bow wave" accumulation
    18тЖТ5. **Man-hour leveling** to optimize workforce utilization
    19тЖТ6. **Cost forecasting** with TCO projection and variance analysis
    20тЖТ7. **Simulation capabilities** for what-if scenario planning
    21тЖТ8. **Unified visualization** via an Infinite Canvas interface
    22тЖТ9. **Service demand validation** ensuring fleet availability meets operational requirements
    23тЖТ10. **Unavailability awareness** integrating corrective maintenance status for schedule validity
    24тЖТ
    25тЖТ---
    26тЖТ
    27тЖТ## 1.2 Project Scope
    28тЖТ
    29тЖТ### 1.2.1 In Scope (MMS Manages)
    30тЖТ
    31тЖТ| Capability | Description |
    32тЖТ|------------|-------------|
    33тЖТ| Fleet roster management | Trains, cars, components with lifecycle tracking |
    34тЖТ| Preventive Maintenance scheduling | C1, C2, C3, C4, Overhaul planning with constraint validation |
    35тЖТ| Run assignment optimization | Daily route allocation with mileage balancing |
    36тЖТ| Mileage projection and tracking | Route-based utilization profiles, 12-year forecasting |
    37тЖТ| Constraint validation | Track capacity, labor, materials, unavailability |
    38тЖТ| Maximo integration | Bidirectional sync for PM work orders |
    39тЖТ| Multi-year capital planning | Bow-wave detection, cost smoothing |
    40тЖТ| Service demand validation | Ensuring minimum fleet availability targets |
    41тЖТ
    42тЖТ### 1.2.2 In Scope (MMS Must Receive/React To)
    43тЖТ
    44тЖТ| External Data | Source | Purpose |
    45тЖТ|---------------|--------|---------|
    46тЖТ| Corrective maintenance status | Maximo webhook/polling | Block unavailable trains from scheduling |
    47тЖТ| Inventory snapshots | ERP/Maximo | Validate material constraints |
    48тЖТ| Service demand requirements | Operations team | Set minimum availability targets |
    49тЖТ
    50тЖТ### 1.2.3 Out of Scope (MMS Does Not Execute)
    51тЖТ
    52тЖТ| Capability | Owner System | MMS Interaction |
    53тЖТ|------------|--------------|-----------------|
    54тЖТ| Corrective maintenance work orders | Maximo CMMS | Receives status updates only |
    55тЖТ| Parts inventory management | ERP/Maximo | Consumes snapshots for constraint validation |
    56тЖТ| Operator scheduling | Workforce management | None |
    57тЖТ| Real-time GPS tracking | OCC systems | None |
    58тЖТ
    59тЖТ> **Critical Clarification:** "Out of Scope" means MMS does not *manage* these functions. However, MMS **must receive** corrective maintenance status to prevent scheduling conflicts. Without this visibility, every PM schedule is potentially invalid on the day of execution.
    60тЖТ
    61тЖТ---
    62тЖТ
    63тЖТ## 1.3 Key Architectural Decisions
    64тЖТ
    65тЖТ| ID | Decision | Rationale | Alternatives Considered | Risk Mitigation |
    66тЖТ|----|----------|-----------|------------------------|-----------------|
    67тЖТ| ADR-001 | TypeScript-First Architecture | Team expertise, single language for 98% of codebase, strong typing | Java (enterprise), Python (data science) | Node.js fallback path documented |
    68тЖТ| ADR-002 | C++ Native Binary for Tactical Optimization | Container density (20-30 pods/node), <5ms cold start, OR-Tools native performance | WASM solver (limited), Pure TS (10x slower) | TypeScript fallback solver, Docker build pipeline |
    69тЖТ| ADR-003 | Dual-Horizon Planning (Strategic + Tactical) | RFQ requires 1-10 year planning; single solver cannot handle both granularities | Single solver (bow wave risk), Manual strategic planning | Constraint aggregation layer ensures consistency |
    70тЖТ| ADR-004 | Soft Constraints with Explainability | Operations cannot accept "INFEASIBLE"; must provide actionable least-bad solutions | Hard constraints only (operationally unacceptable) | Fallback greedy solver, explanation trace |
    71тЖТ| ADR-005 | Material Constraints in Optimizer | PM scheduling without parts availability is operationally useless | Labor-only optimization (cited in reviews as critical gap) | Inventory snapshot integration, reservation system |
    72тЖТ| ADR-006 | Baseline/Drift Architecture | Long-term plans cannot recalculate daily; planners need stable reference points | Continuous recalculation (jittery UX, trust erosion) | Published baselines, drift thresholds, dampening |
    73тЖТ| ADR-007 | Scenario-Based Simulation | RFQ requires what-if analysis; cannot store in single-reality schema | In-memory only (data loss), Full DB clone (storage bloat) | Event-sourced deltas, materialized comparison views |
    74тЖТ| ADR-008 | Infinite Canvas UI (deck.gl) | Semantic zoom from 12-year strategy to daily operations without context switching | Paginated tables (poor UX), Separate views (fragmented) | Progressive enhancement, tabular fallback |
    75тЖТ| ADR-009 | Explanation Trace in Optimizer Output | Operators must understand "why" to trust and adopt system recommendations | Black-box output (user rejection, manual overrides) | Factor-based explanations, human-readable descriptions |
    76тЖТ| ADR-010 | Audit Trail for All Schedule Changes | 12-year government contract requires full attribution and compliance evidence | Implicit logging only | Immutable history tables, user attribution |
    77тЖТ| **ADR-011** | **Unavailability Event Integration** | PM scheduling requires knowledge of trains blocked by corrective work; without this, schedules are invalid on day of execution | Ignore corrective (operationally invalid) | Maximo webhook integration; manual status update UI as fallback |
    78тЖТ| **ADR-012** | **Service Demand as Hard Constraint** | Optimizer must know minimum fleet availability target; cannot validate bow-wave risk without knowing "how many trains do we need?" | Assume 100% availability (unrealistic) | Require service demand input during onboarding; default to 85% fleet availability if unknown |
    79тЖТ| **ADR-013** | **Route-Based Utilization Profiles** | Mileage projection accuracy requires route-specific daily rates, not fleet averages; projection error compounds over 12-year horizon | Fleet-wide average (60,000 km error over 12 years) | Route utilization profiles table; manual override per train; explicit flags for unknown routes |
    80тЖТ| **ADR-014** | **PM History Assumptions for Cold Start** | Data migration must handle incomplete historical records with explicit confidence levels; system must not crash or produce invalid projections from missing data | Assume complete history (crash risk), Ignore missing (false alerts) | Assumption tracking table; inference rules; confidence scoring; manual review queue |
    81тЖТ
    82тЖТ---
    83тЖТ
    84тЖТ## 1.4 Risk Assessment & Mitigation
    85тЖТ
    86тЖТ| Risk ID | Risk Description | Probability | Impact | Mitigation Strategy | Owner |
    87тЖТ|---------|------------------|-------------|--------|---------------------|-------|
    88тЖТ| R-001 | C++ binary build complexity | Medium | High | Containerized build pipeline, pre-built binaries, TypeScript fallback | DevOps |
    89тЖТ| R-002 | Team TypeScript focus with C++ component | Low | Medium | C++ contractor with knowledge transfer, comprehensive integration tests | Tech Lead |
    90тЖТ| R-003 | Strategic/Tactical solver inconsistency | Medium | High | Constraint aggregation layer, cross-validation tests, unified constraint model | Architect |
    91тЖТ| R-004 | Material constraint data quality | High | High | Inventory snapshot validation, fallback to labor-only mode, data quality dashboard | Data Team |
    92тЖТ| R-005 | Operator distrust of optimizer | Medium | High | Explanation traces, gradual rollout, manual override capability, training program | Product |
    93тЖТ| R-006 | Long-term plan instability (jitter) | Medium | Medium | Baseline snapshots, drift thresholds, dampening in projections | Backend |
    94тЖТ| R-007 | deck.gl WebGL performance on low-end devices | Medium | Medium | Progressive enhancement, LOD rendering, tabular fallback | Frontend |
    95тЖТ| R-008 | Data migration quality issues | Medium | High | Parallel-run validation, automated reconciliation, rollback procedures | Data Team |
    96тЖТ| R-009 | Scenario storage scalability | Low | Medium | Event-sourced deltas vs. full clones, automatic scenario expiration | Backend |
    97тЖТ| R-010 | Maximo integration delays | Medium | Medium | CSV interim solution, shadow record system, dedicated integration team | Integration |
    98тЖТ| **R-011** | **No corrective maintenance visibility** | **High** | **Critical** | Unavailability event feed from Maximo; fallback to manual status update UI; alert when train scheduled without availability check | Integration |
    99тЖТ| **R-012** | **Service demand undefined** | **High** | **Critical** | Require service demand input during onboarding; default to 85% fleet availability if unknown; prominent warning in UI when using defaults | Product |
   100тЖТ| **R-013** | **PM history incomplete at migration** | **High** | **High** | Assumption tracking table (`pm_baseline_assumptions`); inference rules from higher-level PMs; confidence scoring; manual review queue for LOW confidence trains | Data Team |
   101тЖТ| **R-014** | **Route utilization unknown** | **Medium** | **High** | Default to fleet-wide average with explicit warning flag; allow manual override per train; route profile editor in admin UI | Product |
   102тЖТ
   103тЖТ---
   104тЖТ
   105тЖТ## 1.5 RFQ Compliance Matrix
   106тЖТ
   107тЖТ| RFQ Requirement | Status | Implementation Section | Notes |
   108тЖТ|-----------------|--------|----------------------|-------|
   109тЖТ| Fleet size (128 trains, expandable to 500+) | ├в┼УтАж | Part 3: Database Schema | UUID-based, no hard limits |
   110тЖТ| Daily mileage ingestion | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Ingestion Service | File watcher with validation |
   111тЖТ| Run assignment optimization | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Run Assignment Optimizer | C++ CP-SAT with soft constraints |
   112тЖТ| PM scheduling (weekly/monthly) | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: PM Scheduler | 12-week tactical horizon |
   113тЖТ| Long-term planning (1-10 years) | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Strategic Planner | Genetic algorithm with constraint envelope |
   114тЖТ| Man-hour projection & leveling | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Man-hour Leveling Engine | Multi-strategy adjustments |
   115тЖТ| Spare parts availability | ├в┼УтАж | Part 3: Material Constraints Schema | Inventory integration, reservation system |
   116тЖТ| Costing module (TCO, variance) | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Costing Service | Labor rates, material costs, shop rates |
   117тЖТ| Simulation engine | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Simulation Service | Scenario branching, comparison views |
   118тЖТ| Guidance and warnings | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Explanation Trace | Factor-based assignment explanations |
   119тЖТ| Maximo integration | ├в┼УтАж | Part 7: Integration | Phased: CSV ├втАатАЩ API ├втАатАЩ Bidirectional |
   120тЖТ| Web-based UI (desktop focus) | ├в┼УтАж | Part 6: User Interface | Infinite Canvas with semantic zoom |
   121тЖТ| Email notifications & reports | ├в┼УтАж | Part 5: API Specification | Notification service, PDF/Excel export |
   122тЖТ| 12-year contract support | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Strategic Planner | Baseline/drift architecture |
   123тЖТ| Strategic simulation capability | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Simulation Service | What-if scenarios with persistence |
   124тЖТ| Component-level tracking | ├в┼УтАж | Part 3: Component Tracking Schema | Mileage follows components |
   125тЖТ| Data migration | ├в┼УтАж | Part 8: Data Migration | ETL pipeline, validation suite |
   126тЖТ| Documentation deliverables | ├в┼УтАж | Part 8: Documentation Plan | User manual, API reference, training |
   127тЖТ| **Service availability validation** | ├в┼УтАж | Part 3: Service Demand Schema | Min train requirements by day type |
   128тЖТ| **Corrective maintenance awareness** | ├в┼УтАж | Part 7: CM Integration | Maximo unavailability feed |
   129тЖТ
   130тЖТ---
   131тЖТ
   132тЖТ## 1.6 Technology Stack Summary
   133тЖТ

   167тЖТ## 1.7 Glossary of Terms
   168тЖТ
   169тЖТ| Term | Definition |
   170тЖТ|------|------------|
   171тЖТ| PM | Preventive Maintenance (C1, C2, C3, C4, Overhaul) |
   172тЖТ| Fixed Parameters | Resource constraints (tracks, man-hours) that bound optimization |
   173тЖТ| Run Assignment | Allocation of a specific train to a specific route for a day |
   174тЖТ| Mileage Leveling | Balancing cumulative mileage across the fleet to equalize wear |
   175тЖТ| Man-hour Leveling | Balancing workforce allocation across time periods |
   176тЖТ| Compliance Window | Acceptable date range for scheduling a PM (earliest to latest) |
   177тЖТ| Bow Wave Risk | Accumulation of deferred maintenance causing future overload |
   178тЖТ| Soft Constraint | Optimization constraint that can be violated with penalty cost |
   179тЖТ| Hard Constraint | Constraint that must never be violated (safety, physical limits) |
   180тЖТ| Strategic Horizon | Long-term planning window (1-10 years, quarterly granularity) |
   181тЖТ| Tactical Horizon | Medium-term planning window (12 weeks, daily granularity) |
   182тЖТ| Baseline | Published snapshot of a plan used as reference for drift analysis |
   183тЖТ| Drift | Deviation between actual state and baseline projection |
   184тЖТ| Scenario | Alternative version of a plan for what-if analysis |
   185тЖТ| Explanation Trace | Human-readable justification for optimizer decisions |
   186тЖТ| TCO | Total Cost of Ownership (12-year projected costs) |
   187тЖТ| Infinite Canvas | Zoomable timeline interface spanning 12 years |
   188тЖТ| Semantic Zoom | Data representation changes based on zoom level |
   189тЖТ| Bill of Materials | List of parts required for a specific PM type |
   190тЖТ| Material Reservation | Soft or hard allocation of inventory to a scheduled PM |
   191тЖТ| **Unavailability Event** | A corrective maintenance event, incident, or external factor that removes a train from service (source: Maximo CM work orders, incidents, or manual entry) |
   192тЖТ| **Service Demand** | Minimum number of trains required for passenger service by day type (weekday, Saturday, Sunday, holiday) |
   193тЖТ| **Utilization Profile** | Expected daily mileage accumulation rate based on route assignment (e.g., NSL = 350 km/day, EWL = 180 km/day) |
   194тЖТ| **Cold Start** | Scenario where PM history is incomplete during data migration, requiring estimation with confidence levels |
   195тЖТ| **Track Capability** | Specific equipment or features available on a maintenance track (e.g., pit access, crane, lifting jacks) |
   196тЖТ| **PM Capability Requirement** | Equipment or track features required to perform a specific PM type |
   197тЖТ
   198тЖТ---
   199тЖТ
   200тЖТ## 1.8 Document Structure
   201тЖТ
   202тЖТThis specification is divided into the following parts:
   203тЖТ
   204тЖТ| Part | Title | Audience |
   205тЖТ|------|-------|----------|
   206тЖТ| 1 | Executive Summary & Decision Record | All stakeholders |
   207тЖТ| 2 | System Architecture | Architects, Tech Leads |
   208тЖТ| 3 | Database Schema | Backend Engineers, DBAs |
   209тЖТ| 4 | Domain Services | Backend Engineers |
   210тЖТ| 5 | API Specification | Frontend Engineers, Integrators |
   211тЖТ| 6 | User Interface Specification | Frontend Engineers, UX Designers |
   212тЖТ| 7 | Integration Specifications | Integration Team |
   213тЖТ| 8 | Operations & Deployment | DevOps, SRE |
   214тЖТ| 9 | Demo Application | All stakeholders |
   215тЖТ
   216тЖТ---
   217тЖТ
   218тЖТ## 1.9 Critical Dependencies
   219тЖТ
   220тЖТThe following external dependencies are **required** for MMS to produce operationally valid schedules:
   221тЖТ
   222тЖТ| Dependency | Source | Frequency | Fallback |
   223тЖТ|------------|--------|-----------|----------|
   224тЖТ| Corrective maintenance status | Maximo webhook | Real-time (or 15-min polling) | Manual status update UI with prominent warning |
   225тЖТ| Inventory positions | ERP/Maximo | Daily snapshot | Assume unlimited (with warning flag) |
   226тЖТ| Service demand | Operations input | Initial + updates | Default to 85% fleet availability |
   227тЖТ| Route utilization profiles | Operations input | Initial + updates | Fleet-wide average (with warning flag) |
   228тЖТ
   229тЖТ---
   230тЖТ
   231тЖТ## 1.10 Change Log
   232тЖТ
   233тЖТ| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
   234тЖТ|---------|------|--------|---------|
   235тЖТ| 1.0 | 2026-01-01 | Initial | Original specification |
   236тЖТ| 2.0 | 2026-01-14 | Review | Added: Material constraints, Explanation trace, Baseline/drift architecture, Scenario management, Component tracking, Audit trail, Constraint aggregation layer |
   237тЖТ| **2.1** | **2026-01-19** | **Gap Analysis** | **Added: ADR-011 through ADR-014 (unavailability, service demand, route utilization, cold start); R-011 through R-014; Scope clarification distinguishing "manages" from "receives"; New glossary terms; Critical dependencies section; Service demand and unavailability awareness in system purpose** |

# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
# Part 1: Executive Summary & Decision Record

**Document Version:** 2.1  
**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT  
**Last Updated:** 2026-01-19

---

## 1.1 System Purpose

The Metro Fleet Mileage Management System (MMS) is a constraint-based fleet management platform that optimizes train deployments and maintenance scheduling over a 12-year contract period. It replaces manual Excel-based planning with an integrated system providing:

1. **Daily mileage tracking** with automated ingestion from CSV uploads
2. **Run assignment optimization** balancing mileage across the fleet
3. **Preventive maintenance scheduling** within resource constraints
4. **Strategic planning** for 1-10 year horizons to prevent "bow wave" accumulation
5. **Man-hour leveling** to optimize workforce utilization
6. **Cost forecasting** with TCO projection and variance analysis
7. **Simulation capabilities** for what-if scenario planning
8. **Unified visualization** via an Infinite Canvas interface
9. **Service demand validation** ensuring fleet availability meets operational requirements
10. **Unavailability awareness** integrating corrective maintenance status for schedule validity

---

## 1.2 Project Scope

### 1.2.1 In Scope (MMS Manages)

| Capability | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| Fleet roster management | Trains, cars, components with lifecycle tracking |
| Preventive Maintenance scheduling | C1, C2, C3, C4, Overhaul planning with constraint validation |
| Run assignment optimization | Daily route allocation with mileage balancing |
| Mileage projection and tracking | Route-based utilization profiles, 12-year forecasting |
| Constraint validation | Track capacity, labor, materials, unavailability |
| Maximo integration | Bidirectional sync for PM work orders |
| Multi-year capital planning | Bow-wave detection, cost smoothing |
| Service demand validation | Ensuring minimum fleet availability targets |

### 1.2.2 In Scope (MMS Must Receive/React To)

| External Data | Source | Purpose |
|---------------|--------|---------|
| Corrective maintenance status | Maximo webhook/polling | Block unavailable trains from scheduling |
| Inventory snapshots | ERP/Maximo | Validate material constraints |
| Service demand requirements | Operations team | Set minimum availability targets |

### 1.2.3 Out of Scope (MMS Does Not Execute)

| Capability | Owner System | MMS Interaction |
|------------|--------------|-----------------|
| Corrective maintenance work orders | Maximo CMMS | Receives status updates only |
| Parts inventory management | ERP/Maximo | Consumes snapshots for constraint validation |
| Operator scheduling | Workforce management | None |
| Real-time GPS tracking | OCC systems | None |

> **Critical Clarification:** "Out of Scope" means MMS does not *manage* these functions. However, MMS **must receive** corrective maintenance status to prevent scheduling conflicts. Without this visibility, every PM schedule is potentially invalid on the day of execution.

---

## 1.3 Key Architectural Decisions

| ID | Decision | Rationale | Alternatives Considered | Risk Mitigation |
|----|----------|-----------|------------------------|-----------------|
| ADR-001 | TypeScript-First Architecture | Team expertise, single language for 98% of codebase, strong typing | Java (enterprise), Python (data science) | Node.js fallback path documented |
| ADR-002 | C++ Native Binary for Tactical Optimization | Container density (20-30 pods/node), <5ms cold start, OR-Tools native performance | WASM solver (limited), Pure TS (10x slower) | TypeScript fallback solver, Docker build pipeline |
| ADR-003 | Dual-Horizon Planning (Strategic + Tactical) | RFQ requires 1-10 year planning; single solver cannot handle both granularities | Single solver (bow wave risk), Manual strategic planning | Constraint aggregation layer ensures consistency |
| ADR-004 | Soft Constraints with Explainability | Operations cannot accept "INFEASIBLE"; must provide actionable least-bad solutions | Hard constraints only (operationally unacceptable) | Fallback greedy solver, explanation trace |
| ADR-005 | Material Constraints in Optimizer | PM scheduling without parts availability is operationally useless | Labor-only optimization (cited in reviews as critical gap) | Inventory snapshot integration, reservation system |
| ADR-006 | Baseline/Drift Architecture | Long-term plans cannot recalculate daily; planners need stable reference points | Continuous recalculation (jittery UX, trust erosion) | Published baselines, drift thresholds, dampening |
| ADR-007 | Scenario-Based Simulation | RFQ requires what-if analysis; cannot store in single-reality schema | In-memory only (data loss), Full DB clone (storage bloat) | Event-sourced deltas, materialized comparison views |
| ADR-008 | Infinite Canvas UI (deck.gl) | Semantic zoom from 12-year strategy to daily operations without context switching | Paginated tables (poor UX), Separate views (fragmented) | Progressive enhancement, tabular fallback |
| ADR-009 | Explanation Trace in Optimizer Output | Operators must understand "why" to trust and adopt system recommendations | Black-box output (user rejection, manual overrides) | Factor-based explanations, human-readable descriptions |
| ADR-010 | Audit Trail for All Schedule Changes | 12-year government contract requires full attribution and compliance evidence | Implicit logging only | Immutable history tables, user attribution |
| **ADR-011** | **Unavailability Event Integration** | PM scheduling requires knowledge of trains blocked by corrective work; without this, schedules are invalid on day of execution | Ignore corrective (operationally invalid) | Maximo webhook integration; manual status update UI as fallback |
| **ADR-012** | **Service Demand as Hard Constraint** | Optimizer must know minimum fleet availability target; cannot validate bow-wave risk without knowing "how many trains do we need?" | Assume 100% availability (unrealistic) | Require service demand input during onboarding; default to 85% fleet availability if unknown |
| **ADR-013** | **Route-Based Utilization Profiles** | Mileage projection accuracy requires route-specific daily rates, not fleet averages; projection error compounds over 12-year horizon | Fleet-wide average (60,000 km error over 12 years) | Route utilization profiles table; manual override per train; explicit flags for unknown routes |
| **ADR-014** | **PM History Assumptions for Cold Start** | Data migration must handle incomplete historical records with explicit confidence levels; system must not crash or produce invalid projections from missing data | Assume complete history (crash risk), Ignore missing (false alerts) | Assumption tracking table; inference rules; confidence scoring; manual review queue |

---

## 1.4 Risk Assessment & Mitigation

| Risk ID | Risk Description | Probability | Impact | Mitigation Strategy | Owner |
|---------|------------------|-------------|--------|---------------------|-------|
| R-001 | C++ binary build complexity | Medium | High | Containerized build pipeline, pre-built binaries, TypeScript fallback | DevOps |
| R-002 | Team TypeScript focus with C++ component | Low | Medium | C++ contractor with knowledge transfer, comprehensive integration tests | Tech Lead |
| R-003 | Strategic/Tactical solver inconsistency | Medium | High | Constraint aggregation layer, cross-validation tests, unified constraint model | Architect |
| R-004 | Material constraint data quality | High | High | Inventory snapshot validation, fallback to labor-only mode, data quality dashboard | Data Team |
| R-005 | Operator distrust of optimizer | Medium | High | Explanation traces, gradual rollout, manual override capability, training program | Product |
| R-006 | Long-term plan instability (jitter) | Medium | Medium | Baseline snapshots, drift thresholds, dampening in projections | Backend |
| R-007 | deck.gl WebGL performance on low-end devices | Medium | Medium | Progressive enhancement, LOD rendering, tabular fallback | Frontend |
| R-008 | Data migration quality issues | Medium | High | Parallel-run validation, automated reconciliation, rollback procedures | Data Team |
| R-009 | Scenario storage scalability | Low | Medium | Event-sourced deltas vs. full clones, automatic scenario expiration | Backend |
| R-010 | Maximo integration delays | Medium | Medium | CSV interim solution, shadow record system, dedicated integration team | Integration |
| **R-011** | **No corrective maintenance visibility** | **High** | **Critical** | Unavailability event feed from Maximo; fallback to manual status update UI; alert when train scheduled without availability check | Integration |
| **R-012** | **Service demand undefined** | **High** | **Critical** | Require service demand input during onboarding; default to 85% fleet availability if unknown; prominent warning in UI when using defaults | Product |
| **R-013** | **PM history incomplete at migration** | **High** | **High** | Assumption tracking table (`pm_baseline_assumptions`); inference rules from higher-level PMs; confidence scoring; manual review queue for LOW confidence trains | Data Team |
| **R-014** | **Route utilization unknown** | **Medium** | **High** | Default to fleet-wide average with explicit warning flag; allow manual override per train; route profile editor in admin UI | Product |

---

## 1.5 RFQ Compliance Matrix

| RFQ Requirement | Status | Implementation Section | Notes |
|-----------------|--------|----------------------|-------|
| Fleet size (128 trains, expandable to 500+) | ├в┼УтАж | Part 3: Database Schema | UUID-based, no hard limits |
| Daily mileage ingestion | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Ingestion Service | File watcher with validation |
| Run assignment optimization | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Run Assignment Optimizer | C++ CP-SAT with soft constraints |
| PM scheduling (weekly/monthly) | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: PM Scheduler | 12-week tactical horizon |
| Long-term planning (1-10 years) | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Strategic Planner | Genetic algorithm with constraint envelope |
| Man-hour projection & leveling | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Man-hour Leveling Engine | Multi-strategy adjustments |
| Spare parts availability | ├в┼УтАж | Part 3: Material Constraints Schema | Inventory integration, reservation system |
| Costing module (TCO, variance) | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Costing Service | Labor rates, material costs, shop rates |
| Simulation engine | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Simulation Service | Scenario branching, comparison views |
| Guidance and warnings | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Explanation Trace | Factor-based assignment explanations |
| Maximo integration | ├в┼УтАж | Part 7: Integration | Phased: CSV ├втАатАЩ API ├втАатАЩ Bidirectional |
| Web-based UI (desktop focus) | ├в┼УтАж | Part 6: User Interface | Infinite Canvas with semantic zoom |
| Email notifications & reports | ├в┼УтАж | Part 5: API Specification | Notification service, PDF/Excel export |
| 12-year contract support | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Strategic Planner | Baseline/drift architecture |
| Strategic simulation capability | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Simulation Service | What-if scenarios with persistence |
| Component-level tracking | ├в┼УтАж | Part 3: Component Tracking Schema | Mileage follows components |
| Data migration | ├в┼УтАж | Part 8: Data Migration | ETL pipeline, validation suite |
| Documentation deliverables | ├в┼УтАж | Part 8: Documentation Plan | User manual, API reference, training |
| **Service availability validation** | ├в┼УтАж | Part 3: Service Demand Schema | Min train requirements by day type |
| **Corrective maintenance awareness** | ├в┼УтАж | Part 7: CM Integration | Maximo unavailability feed |

---

## 1.6 Technology Stack Summary

```
├втАЭ┼Т├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭ┬Р

## 1.7 Glossary of Terms

| Term | Definition |
|------|------------|
| PM | Preventive Maintenance (C1, C2, C3, C4, Overhaul) |
| Fixed Parameters | Resource constraints (tracks, man-hours) that bound optimization |
| Run Assignment | Allocation of a specific train to a specific route for a day |
| Mileage Leveling | Balancing cumulative mileage across the fleet to equalize wear |
| Man-hour Leveling | Balancing workforce allocation across time periods |
| Compliance Window | Acceptable date range for scheduling a PM (earliest to latest) |
| Bow Wave Risk | Accumulation of deferred maintenance causing future overload |
| Soft Constraint | Optimization constraint that can be violated with penalty cost |
| Hard Constraint | Constraint that must never be violated (safety, physical limits) |
| Strategic Horizon | Long-term planning window (1-10 years, quarterly granularity) |
| Tactical Horizon | Medium-term planning window (12 weeks, daily granularity) |
| Baseline | Published snapshot of a plan used as reference for drift analysis |
| Drift | Deviation between actual state and baseline projection |
| Scenario | Alternative version of a plan for what-if analysis |
| Explanation Trace | Human-readable justification for optimizer decisions |
| TCO | Total Cost of Ownership (12-year projected costs) |
| Infinite Canvas | Zoomable timeline interface spanning 12 years |
| Semantic Zoom | Data representation changes based on zoom level |
| Bill of Materials | List of parts required for a specific PM type |
| Material Reservation | Soft or hard allocation of inventory to a scheduled PM |
| **Unavailability Event** | A corrective maintenance event, incident, or external factor that removes a train from service (source: Maximo CM work orders, incidents, or manual entry) |
| **Service Demand** | Minimum number of trains required for passenger service by day type (weekday, Saturday, Sunday, holiday) |
| **Utilization Profile** | Expected daily mileage accumulation rate based on route assignment (e.g., NSL = 350 km/day, EWL = 180 km/day) |
| **Cold Start** | Scenario where PM history is incomplete during data migration, requiring estimation with confidence levels |
| **Track Capability** | Specific equipment or features available on a maintenance track (e.g., pit access, crane, lifting jacks) |
| **PM Capability Requirement** | Equipment or track features required to perform a specific PM type |

---

## 1.8 Document Structure

This specification is divided into the following parts:

| Part | Title | Audience |
|------|-------|----------|
| 1 | Executive Summary & Decision Record | All stakeholders |
| 2 | System Architecture | Architects, Tech Leads |
| 3 | Database Schema | Backend Engineers, DBAs |
| 4 | Domain Services | Backend Engineers |
| 5 | API Specification | Frontend Engineers, Integrators |
| 6 | User Interface Specification | Frontend Engineers, UX Designers |
| 7 | Integration Specifications | Integration Team |
| 8 | Operations & Deployment | DevOps, SRE |
| 9 | Demo Application | All stakeholders |

---

## 1.9 Critical Dependencies

The following external dependencies are **required** for MMS to produce operationally valid schedules:

| Dependency | Source | Frequency | Fallback |
|------------|--------|-----------|----------|
| Corrective maintenance status | Maximo webhook | Real-time (or 15-min polling) | Manual status update UI with prominent warning |
| Inventory positions | ERP/Maximo | Daily snapshot | Assume unlimited (with warning flag) |
| Service demand | Operations input | Initial + updates | Default to 85% fleet availability |
| Route utilization profiles | Operations input | Initial + updates | Fleet-wide average (with warning flag) |

---

## 1.10 Change Log

| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
|---------|------|--------|---------|
| 1.0 | 2026-01-01 | Initial | Original specification |
| 2.0 | 2026-01-14 | Review | Added: Material constraints, Explanation trace, Baseline/drift architecture, Scenario management, Component tracking, Audit trail, Constraint aggregation layer |
| **2.1** | **2026-01-19** | **Gap Analysis** | **Added: ADR-011 through ADR-014 (unavailability, service demand, route utilization, cold start); R-011 through R-014; Scope clarification distinguishing "manages" from "receives"; New glossary terms; Critical dependencies section; Service demand and unavailability awareness in system purpose** |

    95тЖТ## 2.2 Component Responsibilities
    96тЖТ
    97тЖТ### 2.2.1 Frontend (Infinite Canvas SPA)
    98тЖТ
    99тЖТ| Responsibility | Implementation |
   100тЖТ|----------------|----------------|
   101тЖТ| Timeline rendering | deck.gl WebGL layers with semantic zoom |
   102тЖТ| Strategic view | Heatmap showing PM density per train per year |
   103тЖТ| Tactical view | Gantt blocks for 12-week PM schedule |
   104тЖТ| Operational view | Detail cards with technician, track, duration |
   105тЖТ| Baseline/drift overlay | Shadow layer showing deviation from published plan |
   106тЖТ| Constraint walls | Visual barriers at compliance window boundaries |
   107тЖТ| Drag-and-drop rescheduling | Real-time validation with constraint feedback |
   108тЖТ| Man-hour visualization | Column overlay showing utilization with/without adjustments |
   109тЖТ| Scenario comparison | Side-by-side diff view for what-if analysis |
   110тЖТ| Real-time collaboration | WebSocket sync for multi-user canvas editing |
   111тЖТ
   112тЖТ### 2.2.2 Backend API (Bun + Hono)
   113тЖТ
   114тЖТ| Responsibility | Implementation |
   115тЖТ|----------------|----------------|
   116тЖТ| REST API | `/api/v1/*` endpoints with OpenAPI spec |
   117тЖТ| WebSocket hub | Real-time canvas updates and collaboration |
   118тЖТ| Authentication | JWT with refresh tokens, RBAC enforcement |
   119тЖТ| Business logic | Orchestration layer for domain services |
   120тЖТ| Task dispatch | BullMQ job queuing for async operations |
   121тЖТ| Optimizer IPC | Stream-based communication with C++ binary |
   122тЖТ| Audit logging | Immutable change records with user attribution |
   123тЖТ
   124тЖТ### 2.2.3 File Watcher Service
   125тЖТ
   126тЖТ| Responsibility | Implementation |
   127тЖТ|----------------|----------------|
   128тЖТ| Folder monitoring | chokidar watching `/data/inbox/` |
   129тЖТ| File validation | Format, size, checksum verification |
   130тЖТ| Queue dispatch | BullMQ job creation for ingestion worker |
   131тЖТ| Archive management | Move processed files to `/data/processed/` |
   132тЖТ| Quarantine handling | Move invalid files to `/data/quarantine/` |
   133тЖТ| Health alerting | Notification when files stop arriving (>2 hours) |
   134тЖТ
   135тЖТ### 2.2.4 BullMQ Workers
   136тЖТ
   137тЖТ| Worker | Responsibility | Concurrency |
   138тЖТ|--------|----------------|-------------|
   139тЖТ| `ingestion_worker` | Parse CSV, validate rows, insert to DB, handle meter swaps | 2 |
   140тЖТ| `optimizer_worker` | Spawn C++ binary, stream I/O, store results with explanations | 1 per depot |
   141тЖТ| `tactical_scheduler` | 12-week PM scheduling with material checks | 1 |
   142тЖТ| `strategic_planner` | 1-10 year capacity reservation with constraint envelope | 1 |
   143тЖТ| `manhour_leveling_worker` | Weekly/monthly workload balancing, adjustment generation | 1 |
   144тЖТ| `drift_monitor_worker` | Nightly baseline comparison, threshold alerting | 1 |
   145тЖТ| `report_worker` | Generate PDF/Excel reports | 2 |
   146тЖТ| `notification_worker` | Send email alerts and notifications | 3 |
   147тЖТ
   148тЖТ### 2.2.5 C++ Optimizer Binary
   149тЖТ
   150тЖТ| Responsibility | Implementation |
   151тЖТ|----------------|----------------|
   152тЖТ| Constraint solving | OR-Tools CP-SAT with soft constraint penalties |
   153тЖТ| Explanation generation | Factor-based trace for each assignment decision |
   154тЖТ| Progress streaming | NDJSON messages to stdout during solve |
   155тЖТ| Metrics emission | OpenTelemetry format to stderr |
   156тЖТ| Fallback mode | Greedy heuristic when CP-SAT times out |
   157тЖТ| Material validation | Check inventory positions for blocking parts |
   158тЖТ
   159тЖТ---
   160тЖТ
   161тЖТ## 2.3 Critical Data Flows
   162тЖТ
   163тЖТ### 2.3.1 Daily Operational Flow
   164тЖТ
   165тЖТ```
   166тЖТ06:00 тФВ CSV file drop (mileage.csv) тЖТ File Watcher detects
   167тЖТ06:05 тФВ Ingestion Worker: Parse, validate, insert mileage readings
   168тЖТ06:10 тФВ Ingestion Worker: Update train current_mileage, detect anomalies
   169тЖТ06:15 тФВ Drift Monitor: Check against baseline, flag significant deviations
   170тЖТ06:30 тФВ Optimizer Worker: Generate today's run assignments
   171тЖТ      тФВ   - Input: trains, routes, constraints, inventory positions
   172тЖТ      тФВ   - Output: assignments with explanation traces
   173тЖТ07:00 тФВ WebSocket: Push results to connected canvas clients
   174тЖТ07:00 тФВ Operators: Review assignments on Infinite Canvas
   175тЖТ      тФВ   - View explanation factors for each assignment
   176тЖТ      тФВ   - Drag-and-drop to override if needed (with audit trail)
   177тЖТ08:00 тФВ Export: Approved assignments sent to OCC system
   178тЖТ16:00 тФВ Actual mileage updates (if available intra-day)
   179тЖТ22:00 тФВ Optimizer Worker: Generate tomorrow's assignments
   180тЖТ```
   181тЖТ
   182тЖТ### 2.3.2 Weekly Planning Flow
   183тЖТ
   184тЖТ```
   185тЖТMonday 06:00  тФВ Strategic Planner: Run 10-year horizon update
   186тЖТ              тФВ   - Uses constraint envelope from tactical model
   187тЖТ              тФВ   - Respects published baseline (drift threshold)
   188тЖТMonday 06:30  тФВ Tactical Scheduler: Run 12-week PM scheduling
   189тЖТ              тФВ   - Check material availability for T+14 days (hard)
   190тЖТ              тФВ   - Check material availability for T+90 days (soft)
   191тЖТMonday 07:00  тФВ Man-hour Leveling: Generate 12-week projection
   192тЖТ              тФВ   - Identify overloaded/underutilized weeks
   193тЖТ              тФВ   - Propose adjustments (defer/advance/overtime)
   194тЖТMonday 08:00  тФВ WebSocket: Push updated canvas data to all clients
   195тЖТTuesday       тФВ Planners: Review PM conflicts on canvas
   196тЖТ              тФВ   - Drag to resolve conflicts (audit trail)
   197тЖТ              тФВ   - Review/approve man-hour adjustments
   198тЖТWednesday    тФВ Report Worker: Generate resource utilization report
   199тЖТFriday       тФВ Notification Worker: Send weekly compliance report
   200тЖТ```
   201тЖТ
   202тЖТ### 2.3.3 Baseline Publishing Flow
   203тЖТ
   204тЖТ```
   205тЖТUser Action  тФВ Planner clicks "Publish Baseline" on canvas
   206тЖТ             тФВ   - System validates: no critical conflicts
   207тЖТ             тФВ   - System prompts: "This will create Baseline v3.2"
   208тЖТAPI Call     тФВ POST /api/v1/baselines
   209тЖТ             тФВ   - Snapshot current pm_schedule to baseline_pm_schedule
   210тЖТ             тФВ   - Snapshot mileage projections to baseline_mileage_projection
   211тЖТ             тФВ   - Set previous baseline status to SUPERSEDED
   212тЖТ             тФВ   - Set new baseline status to PUBLISHED
   213тЖТPost-Publish тФВ Drift Monitor: Now compares against new baseline
   214тЖТ             тФВ Canvas: Shows drift layer relative to new baseline
   215тЖТ```
   216тЖТ
   217тЖТ### 2.3.4 Scenario Comparison Flow
   218тЖТ
   219тЖТ```
   220тЖТUser Action  тФВ Planner clicks "Create Scenario" on canvas
   221тЖТ             тФВ   - System forks from current published baseline
   222тЖТ             тФВ   - New scenario_id assigned
   223тЖТEditing      тФВ Planner makes changes within scenario
   224тЖТ             тФВ   - All changes stored as deltas (event-sourced)
   225тЖТ             тФВ   - Original baseline data unchanged
   226тЖТComparison   тФВ Planner clicks "Compare Scenarios"
   227тЖТ             тФВ   - System materializes both scenarios
   228тЖТ             тФВ   - Canvas shows side-by-side diff view
   229тЖТ             тФВ   - Metrics: cost impact, resource utilization delta
   230тЖТPromotion    тФВ Planner clicks "Promote to Baseline"
   231тЖТ             тФВ   - Deltas applied to create new baseline
   232тЖТ             тФВ   - Scenario marked as PROMOTED
   233тЖТ```
   234тЖТ
   235тЖТ---
   236тЖТ
   237тЖТ## 2.4 C++ Optimizer Stream Protocol
   238тЖТ
   239тЖТ### 2.4.1 Stream Architecture
   240тЖТ
   241тЖТ```
   242тЖТтФМтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФР         NDJSON Stream          тФМтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФР
   243тЖТтФВ                 тФВ тФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтЦ║ тФВ                 тФВ
   244тЖТтФВ   Bun Worker    тФВ         stdin (input)          тФВ  mms-optimizer  тФВ
   245тЖТтФВ   (TypeScript)  тФВ                                тФВ     (C++)       тФВ
   246тЖТтФВ                 тФВ тЧДтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФА тФВ                 тФВ
   247тЖТтФВ                 тФВ    stdout (results, NDJSON)    тФВ                 тФВ
   248тЖТтФВ                 тФВ тЧДтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФА тФВ                 тФВ
   249тЖТтФВ                 тФВ    stderr (logs, metrics)      тФВ                 тФВ
   250тЖТтФФтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФШ                                тФФтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФШ
   251тЖТ```
   252тЖТ
   253тЖТ### 2.4.2 Input Message Schema
   254тЖТ
   255тЖТ```typescript
   256тЖТinterface OptimizerInput {
   257тЖТ  type: 'SOLVE_REQUEST';
   258тЖТ  request_id: string;  // UUID for correlation
   259тЖТ  config: {
   260тЖТ    max_solve_time_seconds: number;  // Default: 30
   261тЖТ    num_workers: number;             // Default: 4
   262тЖТ    soft_constraint_penalties: {
   263тЖТ      mileage_imbalance: number;     // Default: 100
   264тЖТ      preference_violation: number;  // Default: 50
   265тЖТ      overtime_hours: number;        // Default: 200
   266тЖТ    };
   267тЖТ    explanation_verbosity: 'MINIMAL' | 'STANDARD' | 'DETAILED';
   268тЖТ  };
   269тЖТ  
   270тЖТ  trains: {
   271тЖТ    id: string;
   272тЖТ    train_number: string;
   273тЖТ    fleet_id: string;
   274тЖТ    current_mileage: number;
   275тЖТ    target_mileage: number;  // For balancing calculation
   276тЖТ    fitness_status: 'FIT' | 'RESTRICTED' | 'GROUNDED';
   277тЖТ    restrictions: string[];  // e.g., ['NO_EXPRESS', 'MAX_SPEED_60']
   278тЖТ    home_depot_id: string;
   279тЖТ    current_depot_id: string;
   280тЖТ  }[];
   281тЖТ  
   282тЖТ  routes: {
   283тЖТ    id: string;
   284тЖТ    route_code: string;
   285тЖТ    expected_km: number;
   286тЖТ    required_trains: number;
   287тЖТ    allowed_fleets: string[];
   288тЖТ    restrictions: string[];  // e.g., ['REQUIRES_ATO', 'LONG_PLATFORM']
   289тЖТ  }[];
   290тЖТ  
   291тЖТ  // Hard constraints (must not violate)
   292тЖТ  hard_constraints: {
   293тЖТ    type: 'PM_DUE' | 'GROUNDED' | 'DEPOT_LOCK' | 'CERTIFICATION';
   294тЖТ    train_id: string;
   295тЖТ    description: string;
   296тЖТ  }[];
   297тЖТ  
   298тЖТ  // Soft constraints (minimize violations)
   299тЖТ  soft_constraints: {
   300тЖТ    type: 'MILEAGE_BALANCE' | 'DEPOT_PREFERENCE' | 'ROUTE_PREFERENCE';
   301тЖТ    train_id: string;
   302тЖТ    penalty_weight: number;
   303тЖТ    description: string;
   304тЖТ  }[];
   305тЖТ  
   306тЖТ  // Material availability (NEW)
   307тЖТ  material_positions: {
   308тЖТ    depot_id: string;
   309тЖТ    part_number: string;
   310тЖТ    quantity_available: number;  // on_hand - reserved
   311тЖТ    next_delivery_date: string | null;
   312тЖТ  }[];
   313тЖТ  
   314тЖТ  // PM material requirements (NEW)
   315тЖТ  pending_pms: {
   316тЖТ    pm_id: string;
   317тЖТ    train_id: string;
   318тЖТ    pm_type: string;
   319тЖТ    target_date: string;
   320тЖТ    required_materials: {
   321тЖТ      part_number: string;
   322тЖТ      quantity: number;
   323тЖТ      criticality: 'BLOCKING' | 'DEFERRABLE';
   324тЖТ    }[];
   325тЖТ  }[];
   326тЖТ}
   327тЖТ```
   328тЖТ
   329тЖТ### 2.4.3 Output Message Schema
   330тЖТ
   331тЖТ```typescript
   332тЖТ// Progress updates (streamed during solve)
   333тЖТinterface ProgressMessage {
   334тЖТ  type: 'PROGRESS';
   335тЖТ  request_id: string;
   336тЖТ  percent: number;
   337тЖТ  current_objective: number;
   338тЖТ  feasible: boolean;
   339тЖТ  elapsed_ms: number;
   340тЖТ}
   341тЖТ
   342тЖТ// Final result
   343тЖТinterface ResultMessage {
   344тЖТ  type: 'RESULT';
   345тЖТ  request_id: string;
   346тЖТ  status: 'OPTIMAL' | 'FEASIBLE' | 'FALLBACK' | 'INFEASIBLE';
   347тЖТ  solve_time_ms: number;
   348тЖТ  
   349тЖТ  assignments: {
   350тЖТ    train_id: string;
   351тЖТ    route_id: string;
   352тЖТ    score: number;
   353тЖТ    
   354тЖТ    // NEW: Explanation trace
   355тЖТ    explanation: {
   356тЖТ      constraint_type: 'HARD' | 'SOFT';
   357тЖТ      constraint_name: string;
   358тЖТ      impact: number;  // Contribution to objective
   359тЖТ      description: string;  // Human-readable
   360тЖТ    }[];
   361тЖТ  }[];
   362тЖТ  
   363тЖТ  // Trains not assigned (with reasons)
   364тЖТ  unassigned: {
   365тЖТ    train_id: string;
   366тЖТ    reason: string;
   367тЖТ    blocking_constraints: string[];
   368тЖТ  }[];
   369тЖТ  
   370тЖТ  // Material warnings (NEW)
   371тЖТ  material_warnings: {
   372тЖТ    pm_id: string;
   373тЖТ    train_id: string;
   374тЖТ    part_number: string;
   375тЖТ    status: 'UNAVAILABLE' | 'LOW_STOCK' | 'ARRIVING_LATE';
   376тЖТ    description: string;
   377тЖТ  }[];
   378тЖТ  
   379тЖТ  summary: {
   380тЖТ    total_penalty: number;
   381тЖТ    trains_assigned: number;
   382тЖТ    trains_unassigned: number;
   383тЖТ    soft_constraint_breakdown: Record<string, number>;
   384тЖТ    material_blocks: number;
   385тЖТ  };
   386тЖТ}
   387тЖТ
   388тЖТ// Metrics (to stderr, OpenTelemetry format)
   389тЖТinterface MetricsMessage {
   390тЖТ  _type: 'metrics';
   391тЖТ  timestamp: number;
   392тЖТ  metrics: {
   393тЖТ    'optimizer.solve_time_ms': number;
   394тЖТ    'optimizer.branches_explored': number;
   395тЖТ    'optimizer.conflicts_found': number;
   396тЖТ    'optimizer.propagations': number;
   397тЖТ    'optimizer.trains_assigned': number;
   398тЖТ    'optimizer.soft_violations': number;
   399тЖТ    'optimizer.material_blocks': number;
   400тЖТ  };
   401тЖТ  tags: {
   402тЖТ    status: string;
   403тЖТ    depot_id: string;
   404тЖТ  };
   405тЖТ}
   406тЖТ
   407тЖТ// CRITICAL: Error handling messages (addresses silent failure risk)
   408тЖТ// See Gap Analysis GAP-003 for rationale
   409тЖТinterface ErrorMessage {
   410тЖТ  type: 'ERROR';
   411тЖТ  request_id: string;
   412тЖТ  error_code: 'SEGFAULT' | 'TIMEOUT' | 'OOM' | 'INVALID_INPUT' | 'INTERNAL_ERROR';
   413тЖТ  error_message: string;
   414тЖТ  stack_trace?: string;  // When available
   415тЖТ  last_progress?: {
   416тЖТ    percent: number;
   417тЖТ    branches_explored: number;
   418тЖТ    memory_mb: number;
   419тЖТ  };
   420тЖТ  recovery_suggestion: string;  // Human-readable
   421тЖТ}
   422тЖТ
   423тЖТ// Heartbeat messages (sent every 5 seconds during solve)
   424тЖТinterface HeartbeatMessage {
   425тЖТ  type: 'HEARTBEAT';
   426тЖТ  request_id: string;
   427тЖТ  timestamp: number;
   428тЖТ  memory_mb: number;
   429тЖТ  thread_count: number;
   430тЖТ  branches_per_second: number;
   431тЖТ}
   432тЖТ```
   433тЖТ
   434тЖТ### 2.4.4 Error Handling Protocol
   435тЖТ
   436тЖТ**CRITICAL:** The C++ optimizer communicates via stdin/stdout streams. Stream failures (segfaults, hangs) must be detectable by the TypeScript worker.
   437тЖТ
   438тЖТ```typescript
   439тЖТ// Worker-side error handling (TypeScript)
   440тЖТclass OptimizerWorker {
   441тЖТ  private process: ChildProcess;
   442тЖТ  private heartbeatTimeout: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null;
   443тЖТ  private lastHeartbeat: number = 0;
   444тЖТ  
   445тЖТ  private readonly HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS = 5000;
   446тЖТ  private readonly HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS = 15000;  // 3 missed heartbeats
   447тЖТ  
   448тЖТ  async solve(request: OptimizerInput): Promise<OptimizerOutput> {
   449тЖТ    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
   450тЖТ      // Start heartbeat monitor
   451тЖТ      this.startHeartbeatMonitor(request.request_id, reject);
   452тЖТ      
   453тЖТ      // Handle stream events
   454тЖТ      this.process.stdout.on('data', (chunk) => {
   455тЖТ        for (const line of chunk.toString().split('\n')) {
   456тЖТ          if (!line.trim()) continue;
   457тЖТ          
   458тЖТ          try {
   459тЖТ            const msg = JSON.parse(line);
   460тЖТ            
   461тЖТ            switch (msg.type) {
   462тЖТ              case 'HEARTBEAT':
   463тЖТ                this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
   464тЖТ                break;
   465тЖТ                
   466тЖТ              case 'PROGRESS':
   467тЖТ                this.emitProgress(msg);
   468тЖТ                break;
   469тЖТ                
   470тЖТ              case 'RESULT':
   471тЖТ                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   472тЖТ                resolve(msg);
   473тЖТ                break;
   474тЖТ                
   475тЖТ              case 'ERROR':
   476тЖТ                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   477тЖТ                reject(new OptimizerError(msg));
   478тЖТ                break;
   479тЖТ            }
   480тЖТ          } catch (parseError) {
   481тЖТ            this.logger.warn({ line }, 'Failed to parse optimizer output');
   482тЖТ          }
   483тЖТ        }
   484тЖТ      });
   485тЖТ      
   486тЖТ      // Handle process crash
   487тЖТ      this.process.on('exit', (code, signal) => {
   488тЖТ        if (code !== 0) {
   489тЖТ          this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   490тЖТ          reject(new OptimizerError({
   491тЖТ            type: 'ERROR',
   492тЖТ            request_id: request.request_id,
   493тЖТ            error_code: signal === 'SIGSEGV' ? 'SEGFAULT' : 'INTERNAL_ERROR',
   494тЖТ            error_message: `Process exited with code ${code}, signal ${signal}`,
   495тЖТ            recovery_suggestion: 'Check optimizer logs and input data'
   496тЖТ          }));
   497тЖТ        }
   498тЖТ      });
   499тЖТ      
   500тЖТ      // Send request
   501тЖТ      this.process.stdin.write(JSON.stringify(request) + '\n');
   502тЖТ    });
   503тЖТ  }
   504тЖТ  
   505тЖТ  private startHeartbeatMonitor(requestId: string, reject: Function): void {
   506тЖТ    this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
   507тЖТ    
   508тЖТ    this.heartbeatTimeout = setInterval(() => {
   509тЖТ      const elapsed = Date.now() - this.lastHeartbeat;
   510тЖТ      
   511тЖТ      if (elapsed > this.HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS) {
   512тЖТ        this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   513тЖТ        
   514тЖТ        // Kill hung process
   515тЖТ        this.process.kill('SIGKILL');
   516тЖТ        
   517тЖТ        reject(new OptimizerError({
   518тЖТ          type: 'ERROR',
   519тЖТ          request_id: requestId,
   520тЖТ          error_code: 'TIMEOUT',
   521тЖТ          error_message: `No heartbeat received for ${elapsed}ms`,
   522тЖТ          recovery_suggestion: 'Optimizer may be stuck. Consider reducing problem size.'
   523тЖТ        }));
   524тЖТ      }
   525тЖТ    }, this.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS);
   526тЖТ  }
   527тЖТ  
   528тЖТ  private stopHeartbeatMonitor(): void {
   529тЖТ    if (this.heartbeatTimeout) {
   530тЖТ      clearInterval(this.heartbeatTimeout);
   531тЖТ      this.heartbeatTimeout = null;
   532тЖТ    }
   533тЖТ  }
   534тЖТ}
   535тЖТ
   536тЖТclass OptimizerError extends Error {
   537тЖТ  readonly errorCode: string;
   538тЖТ  readonly requestId: string;
   539тЖТ  readonly recoverySuggestion: string;
   540тЖТ  readonly stackTrace?: string;
   541тЖТ  
   542тЖТ  constructor(msg: ErrorMessage) {
   543тЖТ    super(msg.error_message);
   544тЖТ    this.name = 'OptimizerError';
   545тЖТ    this.errorCode = msg.error_code;
   546тЖТ    this.requestId = msg.request_id;
   547тЖТ    this.recoverySuggestion = msg.recovery_suggestion;
   548тЖТ    this.stackTrace = msg.stack_trace;
   549тЖТ  }
   550тЖТ}
   551тЖТ```
   552тЖТ
   553тЖТ**C++ Implementation Notes:**
   554тЖТ
   555тЖТThe C++ binary must:
   556тЖТ1. Install signal handlers for SIGSEGV, SIGABRT, SIGFPE
   557тЖТ2. Send heartbeat messages every 5 seconds during solve
   558тЖТ3. Catch exceptions and emit ERROR messages before exit
   559тЖТ4. Flush stdout after every message (line-buffered)
   560тЖТ
   561тЖТ```cpp
   562тЖТ// Signal handler example (C++)
   563тЖТvoid signalHandler(int signal) {
   564тЖТ    nlohmann::json error;
   565тЖТ    error["type"] = "ERROR";
   566тЖТ    error["request_id"] = currentRequestId;
   567тЖТ    error["error_code"] = (signal == SIGSEGV) ? "SEGFAULT" : "INTERNAL_ERROR";
   568тЖТ    error["error_message"] = strsignal(signal);
   569тЖТ    error["stack_trace"] = captureStackTrace();
   570тЖТ    error["recovery_suggestion"] = "Internal optimizer error. Check input data.";
   571тЖТ    
   572тЖТ    std::cout << error.dump() << std::endl;
   573тЖТ    std::cout.flush();
   574тЖТ    
   575тЖТ    _exit(128 + signal);
   576тЖТ}
   577тЖТ```
   578тЖТ
   579тЖТThe Constraint Aggregation Layer ensures consistency between Strategic and Tactical planning by computing capacity envelopes.
   580тЖТ
   581тЖТ### 2.5.1 Purpose
   582тЖТ
   583тЖТThe Strategic Planner operates at quarterly granularity and cannot evaluate detailed daily constraints. However, it must not create plans that the Tactical Scheduler will reject. The Constraint Aggregation Layer bridges this gap.
   584тЖТ
   585тЖТ### 2.5.2 Aggregation Process
   586тЖТ
   587тЖТ```typescript
   588тЖТinterface ConstraintEnvelope {
   589тЖТ  period: {
   590тЖТ    start: Date;  // Quarter start
   591тЖТ    end: Date;    // Quarter end
   592тЖТ  };
   593тЖТ  
   594тЖТ  // Track capacity (aggregated from daily track availability)
   595тЖТ  track_capacity: {
   596тЖТ    depot_id: string;
   597тЖТ    track_type: 'LIGHT' | 'HEAVY' | 'OVERHAUL';
   598тЖТ    total_slot_days: number;      // Sum of available days in quarter
   599тЖТ    reserved_slot_days: number;   // Already committed
   600тЖТ    available_slot_days: number;  // Remaining capacity
   601тЖТ  }[];
   602тЖТ  
   603тЖТ  // Labor capacity (aggregated from weekly man-hour projections)
   604тЖТ  labor_capacity: {
   605тЖТ    depot_id: string;
   606тЖТ    regular_hours: number;        // Total regular hours in quarter
   607тЖТ    overtime_hours: number;       // Total overtime available
   608тЖТ    committed_hours: number;      // Already scheduled
   609тЖТ    available_hours: number;      // Remaining capacity
   610тЖТ  }[];
   611тЖТ  
   612тЖТ  // Material capacity (aggregated from inventory projections)
   613тЖТ  material_capacity: {
   614тЖТ    depot_id: string;
   615тЖТ    part_category: string;        // e.g., 'BOGIE_KIT', 'BRAKE_SET'
   616тЖТ    expected_available: number;   // Projected inventory
   617тЖТ    committed: number;            // Reserved for scheduled PMs
   618тЖТ    available: number;            // Remaining for new scheduling
   619тЖТ  }[];
   620тЖТ  
   621тЖТ  // Constraints from tactical model
   622тЖТ  blocked_trains: {
   623тЖТ    train_id: string;
   624тЖТ    blocked_weeks: number;        // Weeks unavailable in quarter
   625тЖТ    reason: string;
   626тЖТ  }[];
   627тЖТ}
   628тЖТ
   629тЖТclass ConstraintAggregationService {
   630тЖТ  /**
   631тЖТ   * Generate constraint envelope for strategic planning
   632тЖТ   */
   633тЖТ  async generateEnvelope(
   634тЖТ    quarterStart: Date,
   635тЖТ    quarterEnd: Date,
   636тЖТ    depotIds: string[]
   637тЖТ  ): Promise<ConstraintEnvelope> {
   638тЖТ    // 1. Aggregate track availability
   639тЖТ    const trackCapacity = await this.aggregateTrackCapacity(
   640тЖТ      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   641тЖТ    );
   642тЖТ    
   643тЖТ    // 2. Aggregate labor capacity
   644тЖТ    const laborCapacity = await this.aggregateLaborCapacity(
   645тЖТ      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   646тЖТ    );
   647тЖТ    
   648тЖТ    // 3. Aggregate material projections
   649тЖТ    const materialCapacity = await this.aggregateMaterialCapacity(
   650тЖТ      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   651тЖТ    );
   652тЖТ    
   653тЖТ    // 4. Identify blocked trains from tactical constraints
   654тЖТ    const blockedTrains = await this.identifyBlockedTrains(
   655тЖТ      quarterStart, quarterEnd
   656тЖТ    );
   657тЖТ    
   658тЖТ    return {
   659тЖТ      period: { start: quarterStart, end: quarterEnd },
   660тЖТ      track_capacity: trackCapacity,
   661тЖТ      labor_capacity: laborCapacity,
   662тЖТ      material_capacity: materialCapacity,
   663тЖТ      blocked_trains: blockedTrains
   664тЖТ    };
   665тЖТ  }
   666тЖТ  
   667тЖТ  /**
   668тЖТ   * Validate strategic allocation against constraint envelope
   669тЖТ   */
   670тЖТ  async validateStrategicAllocation(
   671тЖТ    allocation: StrategicAllocation,
   672тЖТ    envelope: ConstraintEnvelope
   673тЖТ  ): Promise<ValidationResult> {
   674тЖТ    const violations: ConstraintViolation[] = [];
   675тЖТ    
   676тЖТ    // Check track capacity
   677тЖТ    for (const trackReq of allocation.track_requirements) {
   678тЖТ      const available = envelope.track_capacity.find(
   679тЖТ        t => t.depot_id === trackReq.depot_id && 
   680тЖТ             t.track_type === trackReq.track_type
   681тЖТ      );
   682тЖТ      
   683тЖТ      if (available && trackReq.slot_days > available.available_slot_days) {
   684тЖТ        violations.push({
   685тЖТ          type: 'TRACK_OVERCAPACITY',
   686тЖТ          severity: 'HARD',
   687тЖТ          message: `${trackReq.track_type} track at ${trackReq.depot_id} overbooked by ${trackReq.slot_days - available.available_slot_days} days`
   688тЖТ        });
   689тЖТ      }
   690тЖТ    }
   691тЖТ    
   692тЖТ    // Check labor capacity
   693тЖТ    // Check material capacity
   694тЖТ    // ... similar validation logic
   695тЖТ    
   696тЖТ    return {
   697тЖТ      valid: violations.filter(v => v.severity === 'HARD').length === 0,
   698тЖТ      violations
   699тЖТ    };
   700тЖТ  }
   701тЖТ}
   702тЖТ```
   703тЖТ
   704тЖТ---
   705тЖТ
   706тЖТ## 2.6 Key Design Principles
   707тЖТ
   708тЖТ### 2.6.1 Explainability First
   709тЖТ
   710тЖТEvery optimization decision must be traceable to specific constraints:
   711тЖТ
   712тЖТ- **Run assignments**: Include explanation factors in API response
   713тЖТ- **PM scheduling**: Log which constraints drove date selection
   714тЖТ- **Man-hour adjustments**: Record rationale for each proposed change
   715тЖТ- **Strategic allocations**: Document constraint envelope used
   716тЖТ
   717тЖТ### 2.6.2 Graceful Degradation
   718тЖТ
   719тЖТThe system must provide useful output even when optimal solutions are impossible:
   720тЖТ
   721тЖТ- **C++ solver timeout**: Fall back to TypeScript greedy solver
   722тЖТ- **Material unavailability**: Flag but continue with labor optimization
   723тЖТ- **Constraint conflicts**: Return least-bad solution with violation list
   724тЖТ- **Data quality issues**: Proceed with validated subset, quarantine bad records
   725тЖТ
   726тЖТ### 2.6.3 Audit Everything
   727тЖТ
   728тЖТFor 12-year government contract compliance:
   729тЖТ
   730тЖТ- **All schedule changes**: Who, when, what, why
   731тЖТ- **All baseline publications**: Snapshot contents, approval chain
   732тЖТ- **All scenario promotions**: Delta applied, approval
   733тЖТ- **All system decisions**: Optimizer inputs/outputs preserved
   734тЖТ
   735тЖТ### 2.6.4 Stable Long-Term Planning
   736тЖТ
   737тЖТPrevent "jittery" forecasts that erode planner trust:
   738тЖТ
   739тЖТ- **Published baselines**: Immutable reference points
   740тЖТ- **Drift thresholds**: Only re-plan when variance exceeds 5%
   741тЖТ- **Dampening**: Strategic forecasts use rolling averages, not daily deltas
   742тЖТ- **Explicit triggers**: Users control when strategic re-planning occurs
   743тЖТ

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

## 2.2 Component Responsibilities

### 2.2.1 Frontend (Infinite Canvas SPA)

| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| Timeline rendering | deck.gl WebGL layers with semantic zoom |
| Strategic view | Heatmap showing PM density per train per year |
| Tactical view | Gantt blocks for 12-week PM schedule |
| Operational view | Detail cards with technician, track, duration |
| Baseline/drift overlay | Shadow layer showing deviation from published plan |
| Constraint walls | Visual barriers at compliance window boundaries |
| Drag-and-drop rescheduling | Real-time validation with constraint feedback |
| Man-hour visualization | Column overlay showing utilization with/without adjustments |
| Scenario comparison | Side-by-side diff view for what-if analysis |
| Real-time collaboration | WebSocket sync for multi-user canvas editing |

### 2.2.2 Backend API (Bun + Hono)

| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| REST API | `/api/v1/*` endpoints with OpenAPI spec |
| WebSocket hub | Real-time canvas updates and collaboration |
| Authentication | JWT with refresh tokens, RBAC enforcement |
| Business logic | Orchestration layer for domain services |
| Task dispatch | BullMQ job queuing for async operations |
| Optimizer IPC | Stream-based communication with C++ binary |
| Audit logging | Immutable change records with user attribution |

### 2.2.3 File Watcher Service

| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| Folder monitoring | chokidar watching `/data/inbox/` |
| File validation | Format, size, checksum verification |
| Queue dispatch | BullMQ job creation for ingestion worker |
| Archive management | Move processed files to `/data/processed/` |
| Quarantine handling | Move invalid files to `/data/quarantine/` |
| Health alerting | Notification when files stop arriving (>2 hours) |

### 2.2.4 BullMQ Workers

| Worker | Responsibility | Concurrency |
|--------|----------------|-------------|
| `ingestion_worker` | Parse CSV, validate rows, insert to DB, handle meter swaps | 2 |
| `optimizer_worker` | Spawn C++ binary, stream I/O, store results with explanations | 1 per depot |
| `tactical_scheduler` | 12-week PM scheduling with material checks | 1 |
| `strategic_planner` | 1-10 year capacity reservation with constraint envelope | 1 |
| `manhour_leveling_worker` | Weekly/monthly workload balancing, adjustment generation | 1 |
| `drift_monitor_worker` | Nightly baseline comparison, threshold alerting | 1 |
| `report_worker` | Generate PDF/Excel reports | 2 |
| `notification_worker` | Send email alerts and notifications | 3 |

### 2.2.5 C++ Optimizer Binary

| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| Constraint solving | OR-Tools CP-SAT with soft constraint penalties |
| Explanation generation | Factor-based trace for each assignment decision |
| Progress streaming | NDJSON messages to stdout during solve |
| Metrics emission | OpenTelemetry format to stderr |
| Fallback mode | Greedy heuristic when CP-SAT times out |
| Material validation | Check inventory positions for blocking parts |

---

## 2.3 Critical Data Flows

### 2.3.1 Daily Operational Flow

```
06:00 тФВ CSV file drop (mileage.csv) тЖТ File Watcher detects
06:05 тФВ Ingestion Worker: Parse, validate, insert mileage readings
06:10 тФВ Ingestion Worker: Update train current_mileage, detect anomalies
06:15 тФВ Drift Monitor: Check against baseline, flag significant deviations
06:30 тФВ Optimizer Worker: Generate today's run assignments
      тФВ   - Input: trains, routes, constraints, inventory positions
      тФВ   - Output: assignments with explanation traces
07:00 тФВ WebSocket: Push results to connected canvas clients
07:00 тФВ Operators: Review assignments on Infinite Canvas
      тФВ   - View explanation factors for each assignment
      тФВ   - Drag-and-drop to override if needed (with audit trail)
08:00 тФВ Export: Approved assignments sent to OCC system
16:00 тФВ Actual mileage updates (if available intra-day)
22:00 тФВ Optimizer Worker: Generate tomorrow's assignments
```

### 2.3.2 Weekly Planning Flow

```
Monday 06:00  тФВ Strategic Planner: Run 10-year horizon update
              тФВ   - Uses constraint envelope from tactical model
              тФВ   - Respects published baseline (drift threshold)
Monday 06:30  тФВ Tactical Scheduler: Run 12-week PM scheduling
              тФВ   - Check material availability for T+14 days (hard)
              тФВ   - Check material availability for T+90 days (soft)
Monday 07:00  тФВ Man-hour Leveling: Generate 12-week projection
              тФВ   - Identify overloaded/underutilized weeks
              тФВ   - Propose adjustments (defer/advance/overtime)
Monday 08:00  тФВ WebSocket: Push updated canvas data to all clients
Tuesday       тФВ Planners: Review PM conflicts on canvas
              тФВ   - Drag to resolve conflicts (audit trail)
              тФВ   - Review/approve man-hour adjustments
Wednesday    тФВ Report Worker: Generate resource utilization report
Friday       тФВ Notification Worker: Send weekly compliance report
```

### 2.3.3 Baseline Publishing Flow

```
User Action  тФВ Planner clicks "Publish Baseline" on canvas
             тФВ   - System validates: no critical conflicts
             тФВ   - System prompts: "This will create Baseline v3.2"
API Call     тФВ POST /api/v1/baselines
             тФВ   - Snapshot current pm_schedule to baseline_pm_schedule
             тФВ   - Snapshot mileage projections to baseline_mileage_projection
             тФВ   - Set previous baseline status to SUPERSEDED
             тФВ   - Set new baseline status to PUBLISHED
Post-Publish тФВ Drift Monitor: Now compares against new baseline
             тФВ Canvas: Shows drift layer relative to new baseline
```

### 2.3.4 Scenario Comparison Flow

```
User Action  тФВ Planner clicks "Create Scenario" on canvas
             тФВ   - System forks from current published baseline
             тФВ   - New scenario_id assigned
Editing      тФВ Planner makes changes within scenario
             тФВ   - All changes stored as deltas (event-sourced)
             тФВ   - Original baseline data unchanged
Comparison   тФВ Planner clicks "Compare Scenarios"
             тФВ   - System materializes both scenarios
             тФВ   - Canvas shows side-by-side diff view
             тФВ   - Metrics: cost impact, resource utilization delta
Promotion    тФВ Planner clicks "Promote to Baseline"
             тФВ   - Deltas applied to create new baseline
             тФВ   - Scenario marked as PROMOTED
```

### 2.4.2 Input Message Schema

```typescript
interface OptimizerInput {
  type: 'SOLVE_REQUEST';
  request_id: string;  // UUID for correlation
  config: {
    max_solve_time_seconds: number;  // Default: 30
    num_workers: number;             // Default: 4
    soft_constraint_penalties: {
      mileage_imbalance: number;     // Default: 100
      preference_violation: number;  // Default: 50
      overtime_hours: number;        // Default: 200
    };
    explanation_verbosity: 'MINIMAL' | 'STANDARD' | 'DETAILED';
  };
  
  trains: {
    id: string;
    train_number: string;
    fleet_id: string;
    current_mileage: number;
    target_mileage: number;  // For balancing calculation
    fitness_status: 'FIT' | 'RESTRICTED' | 'GROUNDED';
    restrictions: string[];  // e.g., ['NO_EXPRESS', 'MAX_SPEED_60']
    home_depot_id: string;
    current_depot_id: string;
  }[];
  
  routes: {
    id: string;
    route_code: string;
    expected_km: number;
    required_trains: number;
    allowed_fleets: string[];
    restrictions: string[];  // e.g., ['REQUIRES_ATO', 'LONG_PLATFORM']
  }[];
  
  // Hard constraints (must not violate)
  hard_constraints: {
    type: 'PM_DUE' | 'GROUNDED' | 'DEPOT_LOCK' | 'CERTIFICATION';
    train_id: string;
    description: string;
  }[];
  
  // Soft constraints (minimize violations)
  soft_constraints: {
    type: 'MILEAGE_BALANCE' | 'DEPOT_PREFERENCE' | 'ROUTE_PREFERENCE';
    train_id: string;
    penalty_weight: number;
    description: string;
  }[];
  
  // Material availability (NEW)
  material_positions: {
    depot_id: string;
    part_number: string;

  }[];
  
  // PM material requirements (NEW)
  pending_pms: {
    pm_id: string;
    train_id: string;
    pm_type: string;
    target_date: string;
    required_materials: {
      part_number: string;
      quantity: number;
      criticality: 'BLOCKING' | 'DEFERRABLE';
    }[];
  }[];
}
